title: Substrate-wide adoption of .clia bundle suffix grammar + .fmadapter primitive (<slug>.<kind>.clia)
description:
Multi-session arc. Adopt <slug>.<kind>.clia (CLIA = Command Line Interface Assistant) as the substrate's executable-assistant bundle primitive across digikoma, agent, and ghost axes. All three are specializations of .fmadapter (trained LoRA on Apple FoundationModels), differing only by training corpus: .digikoma = tool-trained, .agent = digital-persona-trained, .ghost = human-persona-trained. Each shares the session-schema + .tui envelope.

Full architecture cut authored as insight memory at ~/.claude/memory/.docc/insights/clia-bundle-architecture-2026-05-26.md — that's the canonical reference for this bead.

WORK BREAKDOWN (rough; each item likely a separate session):

1. Author CLIABundleManifestModel schema family (schema-universal/private/universal/domain/substrate/schema-families/clia-bundle-schemas/v0.1.0/). Typed bundle manifest: kind enum (digikoma|agent|ghost|future), slug, version, fmadapter-ref, session-schema-ref, tui-spec-ref, intent-contract-ref, training-provenance.

2. Decide bundle discovery mechanism: directory scan vs typed registry vs substrate-wide manifest. Lean: substrate-wide CLIABundleRegistry record under vaults/, populated by scanner, consumed by dispatch services.

3. Pilot migration: convert ONE existing digikoma to .clia bundle to validate the shape. Candidate: digikoma-git-push (recently shipped, well-tested, small surface). New path: ~/.local/clia-bundles/git-push.digikoma.clia/. Verify spawning + intent dispatch + receipts still work via savepointd.

4. Author agentd as sibling daemon to savepointd (clia-org/.../tooling/spm/agentd) OR design unified cliad that hosts per-kind services. Decide first; build second.

5. Author agent-cli as sibling to savepoint-cli (or extend savepoint-cli with agent + ghost subcommands). Required flags: --persona, --message, --session-scope, --tui-shape.

6. .agent migration decision: do existing identity-bundle agents (carrie, castor, claude, etc.) migrate to .agent.clia by retraining, or is .agent a new parallel-track category? Operator decision required. Migration captures the soft seam between identity-as-JSON + persona-as-prompt + behavior-as-runtime.

7. Training pipeline integration: pattern for ingesting Apple Python LoRA OR substrate smoke-lora output into <slug>.<kind>.clia/adapter/ with full provenance.

8. Shinji Techo ledgers as training corpora for .agent.clia adapters — the substrate eats its own data. Verify corpus extraction tooling against chronicle/journal/expertise JSONL.

9. Substrate-wide rename of installed binaries: ~/.local/bin/digikoma-* → ~/.local/clia-bundles/*.digikoma.clia/bin/<slug>. Compatibility shims at old paths during transition.

10. Documentation cut: CLAUDE.md doctrine paragraphs updated to name CLIA as the executable-assistant primitive (not just a brand); wd skill references updated; relevant identity bundles updated.

NOT to do in v0.1: actually retrain existing agents (gate on (6) decision); ship cliad daemon (gate on (4) decision); deprecate any existing tooling (additive only until pilot proves shape).

This is foundational substrate work. Multiple sessions, several beads-worth of sub-work each. Authored as one parent bead for discoverability + spinning sub-beads is fine as work progresses.
